description: Maps source-document vendors to Balaka journal templates. Read by an agent during the parse step to auto-populate draft transactions. This is a template. Replace templateName and expenseAccountName strings with the actual UUIDs from your own Balaka instance after importing a seed pack. See examples/agent-pipeline/README.md for the substitution procedure.

bankExpense.description: An operating expense paid by direct bank transfer to a vendor.
bankExpense.journal: DR Expense account (per vendorMappings), CR Operating bank account
bankExpense.templateShape: Standard two-line EXPENSE template with a BANK account hint on the credit line. The agent overrides the BANK line via accountSlots keyed by lineOrder.
bankExpense.howToPost: Call POST /api/drafts with the matched template and an accountSlots entry for the bank line (e.g. {"2": "<operating-bank-account-id>"}).

bankFee.description: A fee debited directly by the bank (monthly administration fee, transfer fee, stamp duty).
bankFee.journal: DR Bank fee expense, CR Operating bank account
bankFee.templateShape: Two-line EXPENSE template with a fixed bank-fee expense account on the debit line and a BANK account hint on the credit line.
bankFee.howToPost: Same as bankExpense. The agent matches by parsing the bank statement line (see skipPatterns for items that must be netted before posting).

bpjsPayment.description: Monthly payment of employer BPJS (Kesehatan and Ketenagakerjaan) contributions to the BPJS collection accounts.
bpjsPayment.journal: DR BPJS Kesehatan expense, DR BPJS Ketenagakerjaan expense, CR Operating bank account
bpjsPayment.templateShape: DETAILED template with two named variables (bpjsKesehatan, bpjsTenagakerja) and a BANK account hint on the credit line. The agent supplies both variables in the variables map; amount is ignored because the total is derived from the sum of the two components.
bpjsPayment.howToPost: POST /api/drafts with variables={"bpjsKesehatan": ..., "bpjsTenagakerja": ...} and accountSlots={"3": "<operating-bank-account-id>"}. If the institution pays only one of the two components in a given month, set the other component to a non-zero value in a separate transaction — do not set either component to zero, as DETAILED templates reject zero amounts.

vendorMappings.note: Generic office-supplies vendors. Consider a more specific template (or a custom expense account) if the institution has a recurring office-supplies spend line worth tracking separately.
DETAILED template. Supply both variables in the variables map. See accountingFlow.bpjsPayment for rules on single-component months.

skipPatterns.reason: Internal transfer between own bank accounts. Record once from a transfer template, not twice from both statement sides.
Net against the original fee line before posting. Skip if the rebate equals the fee; post only the net difference otherwise.

operating.label: Operating Bank
operating.description: Primary operational bank account. Source for vendor payments and bank-fee debits.
operating.accountName: <replace with the operating bank account name from your chart of accounts>

payroll.label: Payroll Bank
payroll.description: Bank account used for salary transfers and BPJS contributions. May be the same as Operating Bank for small institutions.
payroll.accountName: <replace with the payroll bank account name from your chart of accounts>

setupChecklist.item: Import an industry seed pack that ships the referenced journal templates (Bayar Beban Cloud & Server, Bayar Beban Listrik, Bayar Beban Telekomunikasi, Bayar Beban Operasional, Bayar BPJS, Beban Admin Bank). The IT Services seed pack ships all of these.
setupChecklist.where: POST /api/data-import with the seed pack ZIP, or via the Data Import web UI.

setupChecklist.item: Resolve each templateName in this runbook to its actual template ID by calling GET /api/drafts/templates on the running instance.
Resolve each bankAccounts entry to its actual chart-of-accounts ID by calling GET /api/drafts/accounts and filtering by account name.

If the institution's BPJS workflow posts Kesehatan and Ketenagakerjaan on separate days, either post two transactions (one per component, each with a non-zero value for both variables where the non-paying component carries the next scheduled amount) or customise the seed pack to ship two single-variable templates. The public IT Services seed pack ships a single combined DETAILED template.
setupChecklist.where: Template customisation via the Journal Templates web UI or via POST /api/templates.

setupChecklist.item: Review vendorMappings.matchPatterns against the institution's actual bank-statement line formats. Add or refine patterns as the agent encounters unmatched documents.
